title: Money arriving while you nap
subtitle: A short visualisation for practising the feeling of income that arrives without your presence.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation practice for when the idea of earning without direct labour still feels abstract or uncomfortable. The scene: a payment notification arriving while you rest.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The practice of visualising passive income arriving is a common application of creative visualisation technique, drawn from general public-domain self-development traditions.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to build the felt sense of money arriving independently of your hours.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation for when the idea of income arriving without your direct labour still reads as abstract. The scene is small and specific: a payment notification arriving while you are asleep in the afternoon.
Sit or lie down. Close your eyes. Take a breath in and let it out slowly.
Picture yourself in the early afternoon, lying on a sofa or a bed. The room is quiet. Curtains are half-drawn. You are asleep.

On the table beside you, your phone lights up. A payment notification. A sale you set up last week, or a client payment that cleared automatically, or a subscription that renewed. The specific mechanism doesn't matter. What matters is this: you are not there. You are asleep. The money arrived without you.
Notice how that sits in your body right now, just as a picture. Some people feel relief. Some feel mild disbelief. Both are worth noting.
Now picture waking up, picking up the phone, seeing the notification. Let the number be a real number. One that matters. Open the banking app and see it sitting there, settled.
Stay with that picture for two minutes. Then open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on multiple lineages; this practice uses the technique in a specific context.
